candidates.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue
candidates.why: Candidate is only one component of revenue/business revenue and is far below the broader revenue aggregation lines; it does not represent total top-line.

variables.notes: Candidate list misses the correct top-line concept. For this financial-services statement, 'INCOME TOTAL' appears to be the best total revenue/income line, aggregating fee/commission income, trading/securities income, interest income, etc. Chosen from full preview outside candidate list. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] INCOME TOTAL
No clear COGS/cost of sales line is present. This appears to be a financial-services income statement where direct production-style COGS is not separately reported.

candidates.why: Clear overhead component above operating profit; personnel costs are standard SG&A.

candidates.row_label: Stock-Based Compensation in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: SG&A-related, but likely a subcomponent of personnel/labor expenses, so including it with parent rows risks double counting.

candidates.row_label: Labor & Related Expenses in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: SG&A-related, but likely overlaps with 'Personnel expenses' and should not be added if parent personnel row is used.
Clear administrative overhead component above operating profit.
Likely overlaps with 'Other administrative expenses' rather than being fully separate; using both may double count.
Residual operating overhead bucket above operating profit; acceptable SG&A component when not a total.
Not an expense line and not from the income statement.

variables.notes: Selected overhead components above operating profit and avoided likely overlapping subcomponents/subtotals such as stock-based compensation, labor-related SG&A, and 'Selling, General & Administrative Expenses - Other' to reduce double counting risk. No separate R&D line is present.
No dedicated research and development expense line is present.

candidates.why: Direct match for interest expense. Best single reported line for finance cost.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit interest expense line as the priority label.

candidates.why: Income statement earnings line, not balance sheet equity.
Comprehensive income line on the income statement, not book equity.
Only one equity component, not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity to holders of parent company
candidates.why: Best match for owner/parent equity excluding non-controlling interests, which is preferred for BE.
Potentially includes non-controlling interests in some years, so less preferred when parent-only equity exists.
Clearly includes non-controlling interests; only fallback if no parent-only equity exists.

final_choice.row_label: Equity to holders of parent company

variables.notes: Preferred parent-holder equity line to exclude non-controlling interests and avoid double counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Income statement comprehensive income attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.
Direct balance sheet non-controlling interest line.
Direct balance sheet minority interest line; equivalent concept.
Income statement attribution line, not balance sheet minority equity.

variables.notes: Selected the balance sheet minority interest line to pair with parent-only BE.
